w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: Dear Resident, Family Member or Representative,
On behalf of everyone at Swan Care Home, we would like to extend a warm welcome.

We understand that moving into a care home is an important life event, and our aim is to make this transition as comfortable, reassuring and positive as possible. We are committed to providing a safe, caring and homely environment where every resident is treated with dignity, compassion and respect.

At Swan Care Home we recognise that every person is unique. We provide person-centred care that reflects each resident's individual wishes, preferences, cultural background, beliefs and life experiences. Our team works closely with residents, relatives and healthcare professionals to ensure care is tailored to individual needs while promoting independence, choice and wellbeing.

Our experienced staff provide 24-hour support in a welcoming environment, encouraging residents to maintain their independence, participate in meaningful activities and enjoy the highest possible quality of life.
If you have any questions please speak to any member of our team.
Treat every resident with dignity and respect
Be treated with dignity and respect
Inform changes to LPA/Next of Kin

Swan Care Home operates Closed Circuit Television (CCTV) within communal areas only to help maintain the safety, security and wellbeing of residents, visitors, staff and the premises. CCTV is not installed within residents' bedrooms, bathrooms, toilets or any other area where a person would reasonably expect complete privacy.
The purpose of CCTV is to:
Promote the safety and wellbeing of residents, staff and visitors.
Assist in the prevention and detection of crime, abuse, neglect, violence or anti-social behaviour.
Protect the Home's property and assets.
Assist with investigations into accidents, incidents or complaints where appropriate.

CCTV recordings are normally retained for 7 days, after which they are automatically overwritten unless they are required for safeguarding investigations, police enquiries, insurance claims, legal proceedings or regulatory investigations. Where recordings are retained for these purposes, they will only be kept for as long as necessary.
Access to CCTV recordings is strictly controlled and limited to the Nominated Individual.

If the Nominated Individual is unavailable, access may only be authorised to another Senior Manager or Director acting on behalf of the Nominated Individual where there is a legitimate operational, safeguarding, legal or regulatory reason. Any access must be authorised, proportionate and recorded within the Home's CCTV Access Log.
CCTV recordings will not be viewed routinely and will only be accessed where there is a genuine business, safeguarding or legal requirement.
Swan Care Home is committed to protecting your privacy and ensuring that all personal information is handled confidentially and securely.

Your personal information will only be accessed by authorised members of staff who require the information to provide your care, fulfil their professional responsibilities or carry out their role safely and effectively.
Information will only be shared where it is necessary and lawful to do so. This may include sharing information with:
Allied Healthcare Professionals involved in your care.
Other statutory or regulatory bodies where required by law.
Information will not be shared with family members, friends or any other third party without your consent, unless:
You have authorised us to do so.
Your appointed Health and Welfare Lasting Power of Attorney or Court Appointed Deputy has legal authority to act on your behalf.
Sharing is necessary to protect you or another person from serious harm.
We are required to do so by law or by a regulatory authority.

All personal information is processed in accordance with the UK General Data Protection Regulation (UK GDPR), the Data Protection Act 2018, the Mental Capacity Act 2005, and Swan Care Home's Confidentiality, Information Governance and Data Protection Policies.
Residents and their representatives have the right to:
Understand how their information is used.
Request access to their personal information.
Raise concerns regarding the handling of their information.
Make a complaint to the Home or to the Information Commissioner's Office (ICO) if they believe their information has not been handled correctly.

I confirm that the above information regarding CCTV, confidentiality, information sharing and data protection has been explained to me in a way that I understand. I have had the opportunity to ask questions before providing my consent.
I confirm that I have received, read (or had read and explained to me), and fully understand the contents of this
I have had the opportunity to ask questions, and any questions I had have been answered to my satisfaction.

I understand the services provided by Swan Care Home, the terms of admission, my rights and responsibilities, the fees and funding arrangements, the consent requirements, and the policies contained within this pack.
I acknowledge that I have been given sufficient time to consider the information provided and that I agree to the arrangements outlined within this Admission Pack.
I understand that I may request clarification at any time and that a copy of this document has been provided to me or my representative.
I confirm that I have read and understood the contents of
I confirm that all information has been explained to me in a way that I understand.
I have had the opportunity to ask questions.
I have received a copy of this Admission Pack.
